title: TNO Leads TAKE-OFF Project to Convert CO2 and Hydrogen into SAF
content: TNO led the European "TAKE-OFF" project, a collaboration of ten partners, to develop technology converting CO2 and renewable hydrogen into Sustainable Aviation Fuel (SAF). Addressing the aviation industry's urgent need to decarbonize, the project created a specific process to produce jet fuel, bypassing the limitations of biological feedstocks and the inefficiencies of traditional Fischer-Tropsch synthesis. The team developed two routes: a direct pathway converting CO2 and hydrogen straight to fuel building blocks, and an indirect route utilizing TNO's SIENNA reactor, which improves methanol yield by 40%. The project successfully produced a proof-of-concept SAF that meets certification requirements and generates four times less soot than conventional fuel. By validating the technology from TRL 3 to TRL 5, TAKE-OFF offers a scalable blueprint to help meet the EU's ReFuelEU mandate of 35% synthetic SAF by 2050.
author: Analysts Account
